claim_status: No-claim sandbox probe-package template only; no sandbox-readiness claim, no renderer-security claim, no site-isolation claim, no hostile-browsing safety claim, no platform containment claim, no packaged-build probe claim, no effective-policy claim, no SEC-GATE-1 claim, no SEC-GATE-6 claim, and no production-safety claim.

package_status.current_scope: Template and rejection rules for a future host-safe expected-deny probe package; it records no executed probe, no effective platform policy, and no owner-approved sandbox evidence.

required_artifacts: AppContainer or token identity, package SID, and capability list captured from the launched role
integrity level and restricted token state captured from the launched role
job limits and child-process restrictions captured from the launched role
process mitigations including dynamic-code policy and Win32k or equivalent restrictions
handle allowlist and inherited-handle inspection for brokered handles only
platform_evidence_requirements.missing_evidence: No Windows packaged role, AppContainer/token capture, integrity capture, job/mitigation capture, handle allowlist, dynamic-code policy, or expected-deny result has run.

required_artifacts: seatbelt or app sandbox profile captured for the launched role
entitlements and hardened runtime state captured from the signed artifact
JIT entitlement state and executable-memory exceptions captured from the launched role
code signing status, team identity, and notarization or local test-signing state
broker descriptors and sandbox extensions granted to the launched role
platform_evidence_requirements.missing_evidence: No macOS packaged role, seatbelt/app sandbox profile, entitlement capture, hardened runtime capture, JIT state, code signing state, or expected-deny result has run.

required_artifacts: namespace map for pid, mount, user, network, ipc, and cgroup isolation
seccomp filter and syscall disposition captured from the launched role
Landlock or portal policy and broker descriptor list captured from the launched role
mount table and read/write path map captured from the launched role
capabilities bounding set and ambient capability state captured from the launched role
platform_evidence_requirements.missing_evidence: No Linux packaged role, namespace map, seccomp filter, Landlock/portal policy, mount table, capabilities capture, broker descriptors, or expected-deny result has run.

probe_lifecycle.required_record: Build the sandbox-probe package from reviewed source and record artifact build ID, compiler profile, command line, and source revision.
Launch each role through the same role runner, handle set, sandbox primitive, and broker policy used by the evaluated configuration.
Attempt the expected-deny file, socket, process, registry, device, shared-memory, credential, debug, profile, or IPC operation from inside the launched role.
Capture effective platform policy, process identity, broker descriptors, capability state, and policy artifact hashes before classifying the result.
Record any brokered exception, authorized handle, unsupported platform case, degraded mode, or waiver without counting it as a denial pass.
Run cleanup for temporary roots, fake profiles, fake credentials, and loopback fixtures, then classify pass, fail, unsupported, skipped, or blocked with owner-review status.

rejection_rules.condition: Reject the package before execution if host-safe temporary roots, fake credentials, fake profiles, loopback fixtures, and cleanup checks are absent.
Reject any sandbox or SEC-GATE evidence if effective policy capture, process identity, and policy artifact hash are absent.
Reject packages that record success paths only or omit expected-deny attempts for required roles and access surfaces.
Reject hidden failures, swallowed errors, ambiguous unsupported cases, skipped probes, or pass records without a failure denominator.
Reject packages that touch real secrets, real credential stores, real signing keys, real profile data, or a production profile.
Reject unbounded or unaudited probes that can mutate the host outside reviewed temporary roots, fake stores, and broker fixtures.
Reject promotion when security and platform owner review is absent, stale, or based only on this no-claim template.
Reject any sandbox-readiness, renderer-security, site-isolation, hostile-browsing safety, SEC-GATE, platform-containment, production-safety, or weakened-policy claim based only on this template.
